title: Genes with known cis-eQTLs show stronger biophysics–expression coupling than genes without — body deformability partial |r| = 0.262 (eQTL+, n=16,692) vs 0.117 (eQTL−, n=820), Δ|r| = +0.145

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Genes with known cis-eQTLs show stronger biophysics–expression coupling than gen'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: GTEx v8 Whole Blood cis-eQTL table (variant_id, gene_id, slope, pval, tss_distance). Used to partition genes into eQTL+ (has at least one significant cis-eQTL) vs eQTL− (none).
GTEx v8 Whole Blood per-gene median TPM as the response variable for the biophysics-expression partial correlations.
Per-gene biophysical metrics over the gene body and the proximal promoter (TSS ± 500 bp). Metrics include deformability, curvature, stacking ΔG₃₇, melting temp, k-mer complexity.

operations.note: Tag each gene as eQTL+ if it has at least one significant cis-eQTL in Whole Blood, eQTL− otherwise. 16,692 eQTL+ and 820 eQTL− genes matched to biophysics.
Per-group partial Spearman. eQTL+ and eQTL− correlations come from the stratified fit.

statistics.name: partial_r_body_deformability_tpm_eqtl_positive
statistics.note: Body-deformability partial Spearman r among eQTL+ genes in Whole Blood. Sign is negative (more deformable body → lower TPM); magnitude is substantial at this large n.

statistics.name: partial_r_body_deformability_tpm_eqtl_negative
statistics.note: Same partial r among eQTL− genes (n=820, so underpowered). Same sign but half the magnitude.

statistics.name: abs_partial_r_body_deformability_tpm_eqtl_positive
statistics.note: Absolute value of eQTL+ partial r for the magnitude conjunct.

statistics.name: partial_spearman_p_eqtl_positive
statistics.note: p = 1.7e-259 at n=16,692; the eQTL+ correlation is not a borderline test.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts license the stronger-coupling-at-eQTL+ claim: (1) |r_eQTL+| > |r_eQTL−| — stat-vs-stat on absolute partial r — establishes the direction of the differential; (2) Δ|r| > 0.05 sets a meaningful effect-size threshold, excluding trivially small differences driven by n imbalance; (3) eQTL+ p < 1e-10 ensures the eQTL+ correlation itself is far from noise — against the alternative that the Δ is driven by eQTL− being underpowered at n=820 while eQTL+ at n=16,692 just measures a small genuine effect precisely. With r=-0.262 at eQTL+, the effect is substantive in both magnitude and precision.
inference.failure_mode: If Δ|r| fell below 0.05 after proper n-matched resampling (drop eQTL+ to 820 bootstrap samples), the underlying claim 'eQTL-present regulatory architecture co-occurs with stronger biophysical coupling' weakens — the observed difference could be power-driven. This n-matched robustness check is open.

conclusion.assertion: Across 17,512 autosomal protein-coding genes in GTEx v8 Whole Blood, partitioning by cis-eQTL status reveals stronger biophysics-expression coupling at eQTL+ genes: body-deformability partial Spearman r (controlling GC and gene length) is -0.262 (p = 1.7e-259, n = 16,692) for eQTL+ genes versus -0.117 (p = 8.1e-4, n = 820) for eQTL- genes — a Δ|r| of +0.145. The material channel (intrinsic biophysical properties of gene sequence) and the regulatory channel (cis-eQTL architecture) co-occur: genes whose expression is modulated by nearby genetic variants are also the genes where biophysics predicts expression most strongly, connecting allele-level and genome-wide regulatory layers.

scope.scale_note: Primary fixture is body deformability, which shows the largest Δ|r|. Promoter deformability, curvature, and other tracks have smaller but directionally consistent Δ|r| values (reported in eqtl_gene_overlap.csv) and live as separate child fixtures.

confidence.note: Partial-r p-value 1.7e-259 at eQTL+; no formal test of Δ|r| significance; n-matched resampling is open.

external_assumptions.statement: GTEx v8 Whole Blood cis-eQTL catalog is a faithful partition of genes into those with / without detectable cis-regulatory variation; classification threshold is the GTEx default FDR < 0.05.

The 820/16,692 eQTL−/eQTL+ imbalance does not drive the Δ|r| observation — at both sample sizes the partial-r estimates are well above the relevant precision floor, but a n-matched bootstrap sensitivity analysis would tighten the inference.

Gene body biophysics and cis-eQTL regulation capture genuinely complementary regulatory layers — the observed co-occurrence is not a hidden confound of (e.g.) gene length or expression level, which are controlled for via partial-r with GC + gene_length.
